infobox.value: is a [handball](/wiki/Handball "Handball") club, based in the two [Danish](/wiki/Denmark "Denmark") cities of [Bjerringbro](/wiki/Bjerringbro "Bjerringbro") and [Silkeborg](/wiki/Silkeborg "Silkeborg"). Currently, Bjerringbro-Silkeborg competes in the men's [Danish Handball League](/wiki/Danish_Handball_League "Danish Handball League"). The home arena of the team is [JYSK Arena](/wiki/Silkeborg-Hallerne "Silkeborg-Hallerne").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Bjerringbro-Silkeborg](/w/index.php?title=Bjerringbro-Silkeborg&redirect=no "Bjerringbro-Silkeborg"))

Danish handball club

See also: [Silkeborg-Voel KFUM](/wiki/Silkeborg-Voel_KFUM "Silkeborg-Voel KFUM") and [Bjerringbro FH](/wiki/Bjerringbro_FH "Bjerringbro FH")

| **Bjerringbro-Silkeborg** | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Full name | Bjerringbro-Silkeborg Håndbold |
| Short name | BSH |
| Founded | 2005; 21 years ago (2005) |
| Arena | [JYSK Arena](/wiki/Silkeborg-Hallerne "Silkeborg-Hallerne") |
| Capacity | 3,000 |
| President | Frank Lajer |
| Head coach | Simon Sørensen |
| League | [Håndboldligaen](/wiki/Danish_Handball_League "Danish Handball League") |
| 2023–24 | 4th of 14 (regular season) |
| Club colours |  |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | [Team colours](/wiki/File:Kit_left_arm.svg "Team colours")    [Team colours](/wiki/File:Kit_body.svg "Team colours")    [Team colours](/wiki/File:Kit_right_arm.svg "Team colours")    [Team colours](/wiki/File:Kit_shorts.svg "Team colours")  **Home** | [Team colours](/wiki/File:Kit_left_arm.svg "Team colours")    [Team colours](/wiki/File:Kit_body.svg "Team colours")    [Team colours](/wiki/File:Kit_right_arm.svg "Team colours")    [Team colours](/wiki/File:Kit_shorts.svg "Team colours")  **Away** | | |
| **Website**   [Official site](https://bjerringbro-silkeborg.dk/) | |

**Bjerringbro-Silkeborg Håndbold** is a [handball](/wiki/Handball "Handball") club, based in the two [Danish](/wiki/Denmark "Denmark") cities of [Bjerringbro](/wiki/Bjerringbro "Bjerringbro") and [Silkeborg](/wiki/Silkeborg "Silkeborg"). Currently, Bjerringbro-Silkeborg competes in the men's [Danish Handball League](/wiki/Danish_Handball_League "Danish Handball League"). The home arena of the team is [JYSK Arena](/wiki/Silkeborg-Hallerne "Silkeborg-Hallerne").

[![Location of Bjerringbro-Silkeborg](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a8/Denmark_adm_location_map.svg/330px-Denmark_adm_location_map.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Denmark_adm_location_map.svg "Location of Bjerringbro-Silkeborg")

![BSV](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0c/Red_pog.svg/20px-Red_pog.svg.png)

BSV

Location of Bjerringbro-Silkeborg

The official fan club is called BSH Support. The fan club has previously been known as BSV Support and as Black Eagles.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bjerringbro-Silkeborg_H%C3%A5ndbold&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The club was founded in 2005, when [Bjerringbro FH](/wiki/Bjerringbro_FH "Bjerringbro FH") and [Silkeborg-Voel KFUM](/wiki/Silkeborg-Voel_KFUM "Silkeborg-Voel KFUM") merged their first teams to create the new club.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The mother club Bjerringbro FH won the silver medal of the [Danish Handball League](/wiki/Danish_Men%27s_Handball_League "Danish Men's Handball League") in 2002. Silkeborg-Voel KFUM and Bjerringbro FH has continued to compete separately with their respective women's teams.[[3]](#cite_note-gennemtiden-3)

In the [2011-12 season](/wiki/2011%E2%80%9312_EHF_Champions_League "2011–12 EHF Champions League") they played in the [EHF Champions League](/wiki/EHF_Champions_League "EHF Champions League") for the first time after losing the final of the [2010-11 Danish Championship](/wiki/2010-11_Herreh%C3%A5ndboldligaen "2010-11 Herrehåndboldligaen") to [AG København](/wiki/AG_K%C3%B8benhavn "AG København").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In [2016](/wiki/2015%E2%80%9316_H%C3%A5ndboldligaen "2015–16 Håndboldligaen") the club won their first Danish championship, beating [Team Tvis Holstebro](/wiki/Team_Tvis_Holstebro "Team Tvis Holstebro") in the final.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The same year they also won the [Danish Super Cup](/wiki/Danish_Super_Cup_(handball) "Danish Super Cup (handball)").[[6]](#cite_note-2016-supercup-6)

  + ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/47/Gold_medal_icon.svg/20px-Gold_medal_icon.svg.png) Gold: [2016](/wiki/2015%E2%80%9316_H%C3%A5ndboldligaen "2015–16 Håndboldligaen")
  + ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2e/Silver_medal_icon.svg/20px-Silver_medal_icon.svg.png) Silver: [2011](/wiki/2010%E2%80%9311_H%C3%A5ndboldligaen "2010–11 Håndboldligaen"), [2012](/wiki/2011%E2%80%9312_H%C3%A5ndboldligaen "2011–12 Håndboldligaen"), [2018](/wiki/2017%E2%80%9318_H%C3%A5ndboldligaen "2017–18 Håndboldligaen"), [2021](/wiki/2020%E2%80%9321_H%C3%A5ndboldligaen "2020–21 Håndboldligaen")
  + ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/89/Bronze_medal_icon.svg/20px-Bronze_medal_icon.svg.png) Bronze: [2010](/wiki/2009%E2%80%9310_H%C3%A5ndboldligaen "2009–10 Håndboldligaen"), [2015](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_H%C3%A5ndboldligaen "2014–15 Håndboldligaen"), [2017](/wiki/2016%E2%80%9317_H%C3%A5ndboldligaen "2016–17 Håndboldligaen"), [2022](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_H%C3%A5ndboldligaen "2021–22 Håndboldligaen")
